config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan tanpa batas, itu mudah berubah jadi pembenaran untuk menekan kebebasan orang. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati martabat dan hak dasar tiap orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru terbentuk ketika individu dilindungi, bukan dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should generally outrank the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily “for the common good” can become an excuse to trample speech, privacy, and minority rights. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every case; public safety, disease control, and basic rules that keep everyone from harming others do justify some limits. The key is that society should serve people, not the other way around, and any restriction on rights should be narrow, transparent, and accountable. If the balance starts from society’s interests first, I think individual freedom becomes too easy to sacrifice.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu jika dimaknai bahwa kepentingan masyarakat selalu harus di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang adil justru kepentingan bersama dicapai tanpa menginjak hak dasar orang. Memang ada keadaan tertentu, seperti kesehatan publik atau keselamatan, ketika hak individu bisa dibatasi, tetapi itu harus proporsional, jelas dasarnya, dan tidak boleh jadi kebiasaan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang paling mampu melindungi keduanya sekaligus.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, history keeps showing that once “the common good” becomes the default justification, it can be used to silence dissent, weaken privacy, and target unpopular minorities. Yes, there are real cases where limited restrictions are justified for public safety or health, but those limits should be the exception, not the starting principle. A healthy society is one that protects individual rights first and only narrows them when there is a clear, narrow, and accountable reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju kalau kepentingan masyarakat dijadikan prioritas umum di atas hak individu, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Yang menurut saya benar adalah kepentingan bersama boleh membatasi hak individu hanya dalam kondisi yang benar-benar perlu, seperti keselamatan atau kesehatan publik, dan itu pun harus proporsional serta bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak ada batas yang tegas, konsep “masyarakat” malah bisa menjadi alasan yang sangat nyaman untuk menyalahgunakan kuasa. Jadi, masyarakat yang adil bukan yang paling sering mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar sambil menjaga kepentingan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general principle. From an American point of view, that idea has too often been used to justify overreach, whether against speech, privacy, or unpopular minorities, and I do not trust vague appeals to the common good without strict limits. I do think public health and safety can justify some restrictions, but only when they are narrow, temporary, and accountable. In my view, a legitimate society is one that protects individual rights first and treats collective interests as important, but not automatically superior.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
